description: Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard

controls.description: Inbound traffic is restricted to only necessary traffic and all other inbound traffic is specifically denied.
Configuration standards are developed, implemented, and maintained for all system components.
PAN is secured with strong cryptography wherever it is stored.
Strong cryptography and security protocols protect PAN during transmission over open, public networks.
Security vulnerabilities are identified and managed.
An access control model is defined and includes granting access based on job classification and function.
All user access to system components is authenticated via at least one authentication factor.
Audit logs are enabled and active for all system components and cardholder data.
Read access to audit logs is limited to those with a job-related need.
Internal vulnerability scans are performed at least once every three months and after any significant change.
